word: H2334 - חַוֹּות יָעִיר - Chavvôwth Yâʻîyr - khav-vothe' yaw-eer' - Havoth-jair

sections.paragraph:
Original: חוּות יעיר

Transliteration: chavvôth yâ‛ı̂yr

Phonetic: khav-vothe' yaw-eer'

BDB Definition:

Havoth-jair = "villages of Jair"
  1. certain villages on the east of Jordan, in Gilead or Bashan, which were taken by Jair, the son of Manasseh, and called after his name; 60 or 23 or 30 cities in total

Origin: from the plural of H2333 and a modification of H3265

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
